children.text: A single-engine aircraft operating beyond gliding distance from shore or taking off from or landing on water shall have on board

answers: one life jacket for each person.
a water activated ELT.
a life raft of sufficient size to accommodate all persons.
all of the above plus emergency equipment specified in the CARs.
correctAnswer: one life jacket for each person.

children.text: A flight crew member aware of a physical disability that might invalidate the license or permit issue or renewal shall

answers: not exercise their privileges unless authorized by the Minister.
commence a flight.
forward the licence or permit to the Minister.
complete the itinerary and then seek medical advice.
correctAnswer: not exercise their privileges unless authorized by the Minister.

children.text: Over a built-up area a pilot may not fly an aeroplane at a distance less than . . . . . feet above the highest obstacle.
ATC may authorize an aeroplane equipped with a functioning two-way radio to depart from an aerodrome within a control zone under day SVFR provided the visibility is not less than

answers: 1 mile.
2 miles.
3 miles.
1/2 mile.

children.text: When over flying an uncontrolled airport to determine the winds an aircraft should be

answers: 2 000 feet above the circuit.
500 feet above circuit height.
2 000 feet above the airport.
any altitude that is clear of the circuit.

children.text: No person shall exercise the privileges of a license or rating unless that person has acted as pilot-in-command

answers: five times within the previous six months.
five times within the previous 24 months.
at least once within the previous five years.
at least once within the previous two years.
correctAnswer: at least once within the previous five years.

children.text: An old style emergency locator transmitter (ELT) may be tested

answers: during the first 5 minutes after any UTC hour.
during the first 5 minutes after any hour.
anytime.
never.
correctAnswer: during the first 5 minutes after any UTC hour.

children.text: The recommended procedure to ensure that the emergency locator transmitter (ELT) has not been activated is

answers: to ask the airport's control tower if they are receiving an ELT signal.
to monitor 121.5 MHz before engine shutdown.
to turn OFF the aircraft's ELT after landing.
none of the above.
correctAnswer: to monitor 121.5 MHz before engine shutdown.

children.text: When activated an emergency locator transmitter (ELT) transmits on
